description:
You are a mobile build-pipeline engineer doing a quick clean-up of inconsistent paths in a CI environment.  
Two symbolic links must exist so every job uses the same locations:

1. /home/user/android-sdk – should point to the real Android SDK folder /home/user/build-tools/android-sdk-r30  
2. /home/user/gradlew      – should point to the definitive Gradle wrapper script /home/user/projects/sample-app/gradlew  

Task objective  
• Ensure that both symbolic links exist and resolve to the exact targets listed above.  
• If a link already exists but points elsewhere or is broken, replace it with the correct link.  
• After the links are in their correct state, create a plain-text verification log called /home/user/link_update.log.

Required verification log format  
• Exactly two lines, one line per link, **no extra blank lines**.  
• Each line must have this exact pattern (including spaces and the arrow token “=>”):

<symlink absolute path> => <target absolute path>

Concrete example of the required line layout (swap in the real paths, of course):

/some/path/linkname => /the/real/target

For this task the required content must be **precisely**:

/home/user/android-sdk => /home/user/build-tools/android-sdk-r30
/home/user/gradlew => /home/user/projects/sample-app/gradlew

Hints  
• “ln -sfn” is handy for (re)creating links without complaints.  
• After creating each link you can test it with “readlink -f” or “ls -l”.  

Completion criteria checked by automated tests  
1. /home/user/android-sdk is a symbolic link resolving to /home/user/build-tools/android-sdk-r30  
2. /home/user/gradlew is a symbolic link resolving to /home/user/projects/sample-app/gradlew  
3. /home/user/link_update.log exists, is a regular file, and its two lines match the format and values shown above **verbatim** (including one terminating newline at the end of the second line).  
4. No root privileges are required, and all work is done under /home/user.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem objects (present before the agent starts):
• Directory  : /home/user/build-tools/android-sdk-r30     (non-empty, owned by user)  
• File       : /home/user/projects/sample-app/gradlew     (executable, owned by user)

State required after successful task completion:
1. Symbolic link /home/user/android-sdk  
   → must exist and resolve (readlink -f) to /home/user/build-tools/android-sdk-r30

2. Symbolic link /home/user/gradlew  
   → must exist and resolve (readlink -f) to /home/user/projects/sample-app/gradlew

3. Verification log /home/user/link_update.log  
   → Plain text, exactly two lines followed by a single trailing newline:
      Line 1: /home/user/android-sdk => /home/user/build-tools/android-sdk-r30
      Line 2: /home/user/gradlew => /home/user/projects/sample-app/gradlew

   File mode may be 0644; ownership user:user.

Automated tests will:
• Confirm both links exist and point at the required targets with “stat -c %N”.  
• Inspect the log file with “cat -A” to verify there are exactly two lines and one final newline, matching the strings above byte-for-byte.
